facts: The patient's name is Matthew.
The patient is a male.
The patient has back pain.
The back pain has been present for a long time.
The patient's back pain worsens with standing.
The patient's back pain worsens with walking.
The patient's back pain worsens with coughing.
The patient's back pain worsens with sneezing.
The patient experiences pain radiating down their left leg.
The pain in the left leg is described as an electrical toothache.
The patient's current pain level is 6 out of 10.
The patient's pain level can reach 13 out of 10 when it gets really bad.
The patient did not take their medications before the visit.
The patient's pain improves with laying down.
The patient has weakness in their back.
The patient has weakness in both legs.
The patient uses a cane to walk.
The patient's foot drags when they walk.
The patient is 30 years post a fall from a roof.
The patient had a possible fracture 30 years ago.
The patient had to stop playing football due to their back.
The patient has been having back pain for about a month.
The patient's pain is located in the lower back.
The patient's pain radiates down to their left leg, hip, and calf, and goes to the big toe.
The doctor performed a physical exam on the patient.
The patient's vitals are good.
The patient has no bony abnormalities on their back.
The patient has no redness on their back.
The patient has no bruising on their back.
The patient has pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae.
The patient has pain with flexion and extension of their back.
The patient has 4 out of 5 strength in their left leg.
The patient has 5 out of 5 strength in their right leg.
The patient's reflexes are brisk.
The patient's motor and sensory functions are intact in both lower extremities.
The patient had an MRI.
The MRI shows a disk herniation at the L4-L5 vertebrae.
The disk herniation is associated with nerve root impingement.
The doctor diagnosed the patient with acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement.
The patient has tried NSAIDs in the past without relief.
The patient has done physical therapy in the past.
The doctor prescribed an epidural steroid injection.
The epidural steroid injection is for the patient's back pain and leg pain.
The epidural steroid injection will be performed that day.
The patient agreed to the treatment plan.
The doctor will refer the patient to physical therapy.
The patient will see the doctor again in about a month.
The epidural steroid injection can take about 2 to 3 weeks to take full effect.
The patient is young and otherwise healthy.
The doctor expects the patient to do well with the treatment plan.
